root_text.an5.143:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā vesāliyaṁ viharati mahāvane kūṭāgārasālāyaṁ.
root_text.an5.143:1.2: Atha kho bhagavā pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya vesāliṁ piṇḍāya pāvisi.
root_text.an5.143:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena pañcamattānaṁ licchavisatānaṁ sārandade cetiye sannisinnānaṁ sannipatitānaṁ ayamantarākathā udapādi:
root_text.an5.143:1.6: Hatthiratanassa pātubhāvo dullabho lokasmiṁ, assaratanassa pātubhāvo dullabho lokasmiṁ, maṇiratanassa pātubhāvo dullabho lokasmiṁ, itthiratanassa pātubhāvo dullabho lokasmiṁ, gahapatiratanassa pātubhāvo dullabho lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an5.143:1.7: Imesaṁ pañcannaṁ ratanānaṁ pātubhāvo dullabho lokasmin”ti.
root_text.an5.143:2.1: Atha kho te licchavī magge purisaṁ ṭhapesuṁ:
root_text.an5.143:2.2: “yadā tvaṁ, ambho purisa, passeyyāsi bhagavantaṁ, atha amhākaṁ āroceyyāsī”ti.
root_text.an5.143:2.3: Addasā kho so puriso bhagavantaṁ dūratova āgacchantaṁ;
root_text.an5.143:2.4: disvāna yena te licchavī tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā te licchavī etadavoca:
root_text.an5.143:2.5: “ayaṁ so, bhante, bhagavā gacchati arahaṁ sammāsambuddho;
root_text.an5.143:3.1: Atha kho te licchavī yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhaṁsu. Ekamantaṁ ṭhitā kho te licchavī bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.an5.143:4.1: “Sādhu, bhante, yena sārandadaṁ cetiyaṁ tenupasaṅkamatu anukampaṁ upādāyā”ti.
root_text.an5.143:4.3: Atha kho bhagavā yena sārandadaṁ cetiyaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdi. Nisajja kho bhagavā te licchavī etadavoca:
root_text.an5.143:4.4: “kāya nuttha, licchavī, etarahi kathāya sannisinnā, kā ca pana vo antarākathā vippakatā”ti?
root_text.an5.143:4.5: “Idha, bhante, amhākaṁ sannisinnānaṁ sannipatitānaṁ ayamantarākathā udapādi:
root_text.an5.143:4.8: Hatthiratanassa pātubhāvo dullabho lokasmiṁ, assaratanassa pātubhāvo dullabho lokasmiṁ, maṇiratanassa pātubhāvo dullabho lokasmiṁ, itthiratanassa pātubhāvo dullabho lokasmiṁ, gahapatiratanassa pātubhāvo dullabho lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an5.143:4.9: Imesaṁ pañcannaṁ ratanānaṁ pātubhāvo dullabho lokasmin’”ti.
root_text.an5.143:5.1: “Kāmādhimuttānaṁ vata bho licchavīnaṁ kāmaṁyeva ārabbha antarākathā udapādi.
root_text.an5.143:5.2: Pañcannaṁ, licchavī, ratanānaṁ pātubhāvo dullabho lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an5.143:5.4: Tathāgatassa arahato sammāsambuddhassa pātubhāvo dullabho lokasmiṁ,
root_text.an5.143:5.5: tathāgatappaveditassa dhammavinayassa desetā puggalo dullabho lokasmiṁ,
root_text.an5.143:5.6: tathāgatappaveditassa dhammavinayassa desitassa viññātā puggalo dullabho lokasmiṁ,
root_text.an5.143:5.7: tathāgatappaveditassa dhammavinayassa desitassa viññātā dhammānudhammappaṭipanno puggalo dullabho lokasmiṁ,
root_text.an5.143:5.9: Imesaṁ kho, licchavī, pañcannaṁ ratanānaṁ pātubhāvo dullabho lokasmin”ti.

translation_text.an5.143:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Vesālī, at the Great Wood, in the hall with the peaked roof.
translation_text.an5.143:1.2: Then the Buddha robed up in the morning and, taking his bowl and robe, entered Vesālī for alms.
translation_text.an5.143:1.3: Now at that time around five hundred Licchavis were sitting together at the Sārandada Shrine, and this discussion came up among them,
translation_text.an5.143:1.4: “The appearance of five treasures is rare in the world.
translation_text.an5.143:1.6: The elephant-treasure, the horse-treasure, the jewel-treasure, the woman-treasure, and the householder-treasure.
translation_text.an5.143:1.7: The appearance of these five treasures is rare in the world.”
translation_text.an5.143:2.1: Then those Licchavis sent a man out on to the road, saying,
translation_text.an5.143:2.2: “My friend, please tell us when you see the Buddha.”
translation_text.an5.143:2.3: That man saw the Buddha coming off in the distance.
translation_text.an5.143:2.4: He went to the Licchavis and said,
translation_text.an5.143:2.5: “Sirs, the Blessed One, the perfected one, the fully awakened Buddha is coming.
translation_text.an5.143:3.1: Then those Licchavis went up to the Buddha, bowed, stood to one side, and said to him,
translation_text.an5.143:4.1: “Please go to the Sārandada Shrine, out of sympathy.”
translation_text.an5.143:4.3: Then the Buddha went up to the Sārandada Shrine, where he sat on the seat spread out, and said to the Licchavis,
translation_text.an5.143:4.4: “Licchavis, what were you sitting talking about just now? What conversation was left unfinished?”
translation_text.an5.143:4.5: “Well, worthy Gotama, this discussion came up among us while we were sitting together:
translation_text.an5.143:4.6: ‘The appearance of five treasures is rare in the world. …’”
translation_text.an5.143:5.1: “You Licchavis are so fixated on sensual pleasures, that’s the only discussion that came up!
translation_text.an5.143:5.2: Licchavis, the appearance of five treasures is rare in the world.
translation_text.an5.143:5.4: The appearance of a Realized One, a perfected one, a fully awakened Buddha.
translation_text.an5.143:5.5: A teacher of the teaching and training proclaimed by a Realized One.
translation_text.an5.143:5.6: An individual who understands the teaching and training proclaimed by a Realized One.
translation_text.an5.143:5.7: An individual who practices in line with the teaching.
translation_text.an5.143:5.8: An individual who is grateful and thankful.
translation_text.an5.143:5.9: The appearance of these five treasures is rare in the world.”

variant_text.an5.143:5.1: Kāmādhimuttānaṁ vata bho licchavīnaṁ → kāmādhimuttānaṁ vata vo licchavīnaṁ (bj); kāmādhimuttānaṁ vata vo licchavī (sya-all); kāmādhimuttānaṁva vo licchavī (?)
